title: Enhanced fusogenicity and pathogenicity of SARS-CoV-2 Delta P681R mutation
type1_summary: The Excel supplementary tables are figure-linked, headered, and directly replot-ready source data. They include epidemiological summaries, curated sequence metadata, and figure-specific hamster/virology measurements.
type2_summary: No clearly identifiable raw/unprocessed instrument-output files were present in the inspected set. The PDFs could not be structurally inspected and are conservatively treated as documentation rather than raw data.
classification_notes: The paper strongly supports the presence of raw experimental measurements in the study overall, but the inspected files are mostly curated supplementary tables/source data rather than raw instrument outputs. The PDFs lack enough structural metadata to classify as type1 or type2, so they were conservatively labeled documentation.

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies the SARS-CoV-2 Delta (B.1.617.2) variant and shows that the spike P681R mutation is a hallmark of the lineage that enhances spike cleavage, cell–cell fusion, syncytium formation, and pathogenicity in Syrian hamsters. The work combines phylogenetic analysis of GISAID genomes, cell-culture virology, pseudovirus and fusion assays, neutralization assays, and animal infection/pathology experiments to link a single spike mutation to the Delta phenotype.

figures.description: Phylogenetic tree showing B.1.617 sublineages forming monophyletic clusters.
Number of SARS-CoV-2 sequences deposited per day in GISAID for India, the UK, and the world.
Daily percentage of B.1.617 sublineages among deposited sequences over time in India, the UK, and the world.
Top amino-acid replacements in spike across B.1.617 and sublineages, highlighting conservation of P681R and L452R.
Growth kinetics of B.1.617.2/Delta versus B.1.1 in Vero, VeroE6/TMPRSS2, and Calu-3 cells measured by RT-qPCR.
Syncytium formation in infected cells, including representative images and size distributions of floating syncytia.
Viral RNA levels in oral swabs from infected hamsters over time.
Body weight changes in hamsters infected with B.1.617.2/Delta or B.1.1.
Representative H&E-stained lung sections from infected and uninfected hamsters.
Histopathological scoring of lung lesions over time.
Area of large type II pneumocytes in hamster lungs at 5 d.p.i.
Growth kinetics of reverse-genetics-generated D614G and D614G/P681R viruses in cell culture.
Floating syncytia formed by D614G versus D614G/P681R viruses and their size distributions.
GFP-expressing adherent syncytia in infected cells and their size distributions.
Western blot of spike cleavage showing increased S2 cleavage with P681R.
Cell-based fusion assay comparing D614G and D614G/P681R spike-mediated fusion with ACE2 or ACE2/TMPRSS2 target cells.
Body weight changes in hamsters infected with D614G or D614G/P681R viruses.
Pulmonary function (PenH) in infected hamsters over time.
Virus titers in lungs and nasal turbinates at 3 and 7 d.p.i.
Representative lung histopathology images showing bronchioloalveolar epithelial hyperplasia and type II pneumocyte prominence.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: Raw measurements include SARS-CoV-2 genome sequences downloaded from GISAID; RT-qPCR cycle-derived viral RNA quantification from cell supernatants, oral swabs, and nasal epithelial cultures; microscopy images of syncytia and GFP-positive cells; Western blot images; neutralization assay readouts; hamster body weights; PenH plethysmography traces/values; plaque assay titers from lungs and nasal turbinates; H&E and immunohistochemistry histology images; and microCT images/severity scores.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states that any methods, additional references, Nature Research reporting summaries, source data, extended data, supplementary information, acknowledgements, peer review information, details of author contributions and competing interests, and statements of data and code availability are available at the DOI link. Figure captions explicitly say that raw data are summarized in Supplementary Tables 1 and 2 for Fig. 1. The text also indicates use of GISAID genomes and deposited sequence metadata as the basis for phylogenetic/epidemic analyses.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: The paper clearly describes collection of raw experimental measurements, but the excerpt does not specify a public raw-data repository or file formats. Raw inputs include GISAID genome sequences, cell-culture and animal assay measurements, microscopy images, and pathology images. For the phylogenetic/epidemic analysis, the genomes and corresponding metadata were downloaded from GISAID as of 31 May 2021.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: Processed data include summarized lineage counts and percentages over time, top spike amino-acid replacement frequencies, quantified syncytium sizes, viral RNA copy numbers, body-weight percentages, histopathology scores, area measurements of large type II pneumocytes, fusion activity values, S2/(S+S2) ratios, neutralization comparisons, PenH summaries, and virus titers. Supplementary Tables 1 and 2 are explicitly cited as summaries of the raw data for Fig. 1.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-specific data are clearly provided at least in summarized form: Fig. 1 captions state that raw data are summarized in Supplementary Tables 1 and 2. Several figures mention raw data in extended data figures (for example, Fig. 2g refers to raw data in Extended Data Fig. 5b). The excerpt does not explicitly state whether a separate source-data file is provided for each figure, but Nature articles commonly provide source data; here that is not directly confirmed in the text.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code files are mentioned in the excerpt. The paper only states that code availability information is available online, but no specific scripts are described in the provided text.
dataset_characterization.notes: The dataset is a mixed experimental and computational dataset. The computational component uses large-scale public sequence data from GISAID and produces processed phylogenetic/time-series summaries. The experimental component spans virology, cell biology, animal pathology, and imaging. The paper distinguishes raw measurements from summarized/processed values in captions, but the excerpt does not provide explicit file names, repository accession numbers for source data, or code/script locations.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The paper reports direct measurements from RT-qPCR, microscopy, Western blots, neutralization assays, hamster body weights, PenH, plaque assays, histology, and microCT. It also says genomes were downloaded from GISAID and that raw data are summarized in Supplementary Tables 1 and 2.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: Figure captions explicitly state that raw data are summarized in Supplementary Tables 1 and 2, and Extended Data figures are referenced as containing raw data for some analyses.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “Any methods, additional references, Nature Research reporting summaries, source data, extended data, supplementary information ... and statements of data and code availability are available at https://doi.org/10.1038/s41586-021-04266-9.”; “The raw data are summarized in Supplementary Table 1.”; “The raw data are summarized in Supplementary Table 2.”; “Raw data are shown in Extended Data Fig. 5b.”
